questions.q: In which field of study did Harish-Chandra make his most celebrated contributions?
questions.explanation: Harish-Chandra was a mathematician renowned for his work in representation theory and harmonic analysis.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra is regarded as one of the great architects of which area of mathematics?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as a founder of the modern representation theory of semisimple Lie groups.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra was born in which country?
questions.explanation: Harish-Chandra was born in 1923 in Kanpur, in present-day Uttar Pradesh, India.

questions.q: Before turning fully to mathematics, Harish-Chandra was trained as a what?
questions.explanation: He began his career as a theoretical physicist before switching to pure mathematics.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra did his doctoral work under which famous physicist?
questions.explanation: He earned his PhD at Cambridge under the supervision of Paul Dirac.

questions.q: Much of Harish-Chandra's work concerns the harmonic analysis and representations of which kind of groups?
questions.explanation: His central achievements lie in the harmonic analysis and representation theory of Lie groups.

questions.q: At which prestigious institution did Harish-Chandra spend much of his career in the United States?
questions.explanation: He was a professor at the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton, New Jersey.

questions.q: In which century did Harish-Chandra carry out his major mathematical work?
questions.explanation: Harish-Chandra worked in the 20th century, with his peak contributions from the 1950s through the 1970s.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra completed his PhD at which university?
questions.explanation: He received his doctorate from the University of Cambridge in 1947.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra is best described as which kind of mathematician?
questions.explanation: He was a pure mathematician working on abstract structures in analysis and algebra.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra completed his undergraduate and master's studies at which Indian institution?
questions.explanation: He studied at the University of Allahabad, earning his master's degree there before moving to Cambridge.

questions.q: The 'Harish-Chandra isomorphism' describes the center of which mathematical object?
options: A polynomial ring over the integers
questions.explanation: The Harish-Chandra isomorphism identifies the center of the universal enveloping algebra of a semisimple Lie algebra.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra was awarded the Cole Prize in Algebra by which organization?
questions.explanation: He received the American Mathematical Society's Cole Prize in Algebra in 1954.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra developed a celebrated formula for the characters of representations of semisimple Lie groups. This is known as the:
questions.explanation: His work produced the Harish-Chandra character formula for representations of semisimple Lie groups.

questions.q: Which physicist, who supervised Harish-Chandra, is famous for the equation describing the electron?
questions.explanation: Paul Dirac, who supervised Harish-Chandra, is renowned for the Dirac equation of the electron.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra was elected a Fellow of which famous British scientific society?
questions.explanation: He was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society of London in 1973.

questions.q: A major theme of Harish-Chandra's work was extending harmonic analysis from real numbers to which setting?
questions.explanation: He generalized classical Fourier (harmonic) analysis to non-commutative semisimple Lie groups.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra's deep results on Lie groups became foundational for which influential modern research program in mathematics?
questions.explanation: His representation theory of reductive groups is a cornerstone of the Langlands program in number theory and automorphic forms.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra spent the final decades of his career mainly in which country?
questions.explanation: He settled in the United States, working at the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra co-authored a seminal paper on arithmetic subgroups of algebraic groups with which mathematician at the Institute for Advanced Study?
questions.explanation: Harish-Chandra and Armand Borel co-authored an influential 1962 paper on arithmetic subgroups of algebraic groups.

questions.q: In what decade was Harish-Chandra born?
questions.explanation: Harish-Chandra was born in 1923, in the 1920s.

questions.q: A research institute in Allahabad (Prayagraj), India, devoted to mathematics and physics is named in his honor. It is the:
questions.explanation: The Harish-Chandra Research Institute (HRI) in Prayagraj is named after him.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra's celebrated 'Plancherel formula' for semisimple Lie groups gives an explicit decomposition of which space?
options: The regular representation (square-integrable functions on the group)
questions.explanation: His Plancherel formula explicitly decomposes the regular representation, i.e. L-squared functions on a semisimple Lie group.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra introduced an important class of representations that occur discretely in the Plancherel decomposition. These are called:
questions.explanation: He classified the discrete series representations, which appear discretely (with positive measure as atoms) in the Plancherel formula.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra's work showed that the characters of irreducible representations of a semisimple Lie group, though initially distributions, are actually represented by what kind of function?
options: A locally integrable function (analytic on the regular set)
questions.explanation: He proved characters are locally integrable functions that are real-analytic on the regular semisimple elements.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra introduced a key tool in his harmonic analysis for studying asymptotic behavior of matrix coefficients. It is widely known as the:
questions.explanation: The Harish-Chandra c-function appears in the Plancherel measure and governs asymptotics of spherical functions.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra defined a class of well-behaved functions central to his analysis on semisimple groups, now named after him. They are the:
questions.explanation: The Harish-Chandra Schwartz space provides the rapidly decreasing functions used to formulate the Plancherel theorem.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra spent his earliest research period working under another notable physicist in India before going to Cambridge. Who was this mentor associated with cosmic-ray and theoretical physics?
questions.explanation: Early in his career he worked as a research assistant to Homi J. Bhabha in Bangalore.

questions.q: The 'Harish-Chandra homomorphism' sends an element of the center of the enveloping algebra to a polynomial that is invariant under the action of which group?
options: The symmetric group of all permutations
The general linear group over the reals
questions.explanation: The Harish-Chandra homomorphism maps onto the Weyl-group-invariant polynomials on the Cartan subalgebra.

questions.q: Harish-Chandra's monumental program reduced the harmonic analysis of a general semisimple Lie group largely to understanding which special subclass of representations attached to cuspidal data?
options: The discrete series of Levi (parabolic) subgroups
questions.explanation: Via parabolic induction from cuspidal data, he reduced the full Plancherel formula to the discrete series of Levi subgroups.
